page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.m1 server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and runs on the KVM hypervisor. The instance features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 8 vCPUs. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures that compute resources are not shared with other tenants, offering predictable processing capabilities.

The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning storage must be attached externally, and workloads requiring hardware acceleration are not supported. There are also no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.m1.xlarge is designed for general-purpose workloads. It is suitable for applications such as medium-traffic web servers, application servers, and development or testing environments that require dedicated CPU resources without the need for local storage or specialized GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance within the ecs.m1 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, delivering 8 vCPUs with dedicated CPU allocation to ensure predictable performance. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring external block storage attachments for data persistence. Lacking specialized hardware accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this instance is designed for standard workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation prevents resource contention, making it a reliable option for consistent execution. The balanced resource profile makes it suitable for general-purpose applications, including web serving, application hosting, and development environments requiring dedicated compute resources.
